content:
- Fixed the hosted workflow parse failure by moving the per-lane published-image ref file off `runner.temp` in job-level env and onto a workspace-scoped path accepted by GitHub Actions, then re-ran `make check` and `make test` successfully.
- Captured real hosted evidence from GitHub Actions run `24642282707` for commit `05d0359df58589e6062616797a6ffcaf6503bf07`: success from `2026-04-19T23:57:54Z` to `2026-04-20T00:07:40Z`, about `9m 46s`, with native `ubuntu-24.04-arm` publish lanes.
- Updated the task and plan files to completed/pass=true with the hosted evidence, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, and confirmed the task switch completed.
- Next if resuming unexpectedly: review final git status, create the required `task finished ...` commit including the hosted evidence and local test results, push, and stop.

- Read the active Ralph backlog, refreshed `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, and confirmed there are zero active failing bug tasks.
- Determined the actual repository stack from code and build files: Rust workspace (`crates/runner`, `crates/setup-sql`, `crates/ingest-contract`) with `make check|test|lint` mapped to Cargo, plus a Go verify-service subtree under `cockroachdb_molt/molt`.
- Resolved blockers from the done list: story 21 task `02b` is completed with `<passes>true</passes>`, so story 21 tasks `01` and `08` are now unblocked.
- Inspected the current publish workflow and workflow contract tests. The repo already has a real three-image `publish-images` workflow, and the contract tests currently assert that `make test-long` must NOT run yet, which identifies story 21 task `03` as the sharpest remaining safety gap.
- Next if quitting now: keep `.ralph/current_task.txt` pointed at `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/03-task-require-make-test-long-to-pass-before-any-image-publish.md` and execute that task with TDD.

- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` with `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/03-task-require-make-test-long-to-pass-before-any-image-publish.md`.
- Selection rationale: no active failing bugs remained; story 21 task `02b` already passed; the current repository already has the three-image publish workflow; and the workflow contract tests explicitly encode that `make test-long` is not yet required, making task `03` the most direct next correctness gate before more publish and novice-user work.
- Next after quitting immediately: execute story 21 task `03` with the `tdd` skill, update workflow contract tests first, then make `.github/workflows/publish-images.yml` require `make test-long` before any publish job can run, and verify `make check`, `make test`, `make lint`, and `make test-long` all pass.

- Read prior Ralph progress and confirmed the current task pointer is `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/03-task-require-make-test-long-to-pass-before-any-image-publish.md`.
- Verified this task is not yet planned or executable: the task file is still `<status>not_started</status> <passes>false</passes>` and there is no task-specific plan file yet.
- Determined that this turn should be planning-only per the Ralph workflow. Next: inspect the current publish workflow and workflow contract tests, then write a plan file for task 03 using the `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` guidance and end it with `NOW EXECUTE` before stopping.

- Read the current publish workflow, CI contract tests, Makefile lanes, and the `improve-code-boundaries` smell guidance before touching anything.
- Confirmed task 03 was still pre-execution: no plan existed, `publish-image` still depended only on a mixed `validate` job, and the workflow contract still explicitly forbade `make test-long`.
- Added the plan file `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/03-task-require-make-test-long-to-pass-before-any-image-publish_plans/2026-04-20-test-long-publish-gate-plan.md` and attached it to the task file.
- The plan ends in `NOW EXECUTE` and chooses an explicit `validate-fast` plus `validate-long` pre-publish topology, with TDD slices to replace the obsolete fast-only contract and keep the long-lane gate visible in the workflow graph.
- Next after quitting immediately: execute that plan strictly via TDD, update the workflow/tests/docs, run `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`, then only if all pass update the task to completed, switch task, commit, and push.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and the required `improve-code-boundaries` and `tdd` skill files.
- Confirmed current task is story-21 task 03 and previous context says a task-specific plan already exists and ends in `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next: read the task markdown, locate the attached plan, inspect the relevant workflow/tests, then execute strictly via TDD if the plan state is still `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Started execution of story-21 task 03 from the attached `NOW EXECUTE` plan.
- Read the current publish workflow, CI contract test, README CI publish safety section, and the workflow contract helper to identify the obsolete fast-only `validate` boundary and the hard-coded `make test-long` prohibition.
- Added the first RED contract in `crates/runner/tests/ci_contract.rs` plus helper support, fixed one helper compile bug, and confirmed the focused test now fails for the right reason: `workflow should define a validate-fast job`.
- Next: implement the explicit `validate-fast` and `validate-long` workflow topology, update helper assertions/tests/docs to the new boundary, then continue with focused red-green runs before full repo checks.

- Implemented the workflow green step by replacing the mixed `validate` job with explicit `validate-fast` and `validate-long` jobs in `.github/workflows/publish-images.yml` and making `publish-image` depend on both.
- Refactored `crates/runner/tests/support/github_workflow_contract.rs` to own the new validation topology honestly: permissions, checkout hardening, publish gating, cache reuse, install steps, and README contract now speak in terms of the fast/long validation boundaries.
- Updated `crates/runner/tests/ci_contract.rs` to use the centralized validation-lane assertion and updated `README.md` so the CI publish safety section documents the hard `make test-long` gate.
- Verified the full `cargo test --workspace --test ci_contract` suite passes.
- Next: run `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`; then update the task/plan files, switch task, commit, and push if everything is green.

- `make check` and `make lint` were green, and `make test` exposed a real test-harness port-ownership race in the Postgres contract helpers. Fixed that in bootstrap/reconcile/webhook contract harnesses by verifying the responding cluster owns the temp data directory before proceeding.
- `make test` then exposed a README operator-doc contract issue; updated the CI publish safety wording to describe the fast/long gate without teaching contributor-only `make` commands.
- `make test-long` exposed three ignored-lane failures. Fixed two by strengthening `DefaultBootstrapHarness` so verify-image convergence is not treated as complete until tracking state is fully reconciled, and fixed the multi-mapping source-command audit to use the honest six-command bootstrap boundary.
- Verified all three previously failing long-lane tests pass individually.
- Next: rerun the full required gate set (`make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, `make test-long`) after the latest harness changes, then update task/plan/task-switch/git state only if the entire set is green.
